title: Uber
content: I took my first Uber ride when I was in Hong Kong earlier this month. I didn’t flag the “red taxi” because it was during afternoon rush hour and I had important business to attend. I didn’t want to be late. So I opened up Uber.app and requested the **Closet Uber**. The closest one happened to be a van. I didn’t wait long until the car arrived. There was no call-to-confirm from the driver though. The traffic was quite heavy so the driver asked to take a longer route. Fine with me. The ride was about 15 minutes and the total fare was HK$86. Next morning, I flagged the “red taxi” from the hotel to the same location and the fare was about HK$45. So Uber costs about twice as much as regular taxi. Same here. I think I’m OK with taking Uber elsewhere. But I would think twice before hailing an Uber in Bangkok.
